response.justification: This candidate does not meet the mandatory technical requirements for the role. Most critically, their C++ experience is significantly outdated (last used in 2015, 11 years ago) with very limited scope, and their career has shifted toward Node.js/TypeScript web development and infrastructure tooling. They entirely lack the required financial services domain expertise—no Fixed Income, Investment Banking, or Capital Markets background. While they demonstrate strong communication skills and some desirable experience (Java, CI/CD), these cannot compensate for the fundamental gaps in current C++ proficiency and financial domain knowledge that the role requires.
strengths: Excellent communication and teaching abilities demonstrated through conference presentations, workshops, and educational content creation
20+ years of professional software development across multiple domains and platforms
Java development background and CI/CD infrastructure experience (both desirable skills for the role)
concerns: C++ experience is severely outdated—last used in 2015 (11 years ago) with minimal scope at National Instruments
No Fixed Income, Investment Banking, Capital Markets, or financial services domain experience—a mandatory requirement
Career trajectory shows consistent movement away from systems/low-level programming toward web development and infrastructure, opposite to role requirements
